import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut current = ""
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(c)
      if c == 32 or c == 9 or c == 10 or c == 13
        if not string.is_empty(current)
          match string.parse_int(current)
          when Some(v)
            result = list.push(result, v)
          when None
            ()
          end
          current = ""
        end
      else
        current = "#{current}#{string.from_byte(c)}"
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if not string.is_empty(current)
    match string.parse_int(current)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn find_pair(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<String>
  let n = xs.len()
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    mut j = i + 1
    while j < n
      match xs.get(i)
      when Some(a)
        match xs.get(j)
        when Some(b)
          if a + b == t
            return Some("#{i},#{j}")
          end
        when None
          ()
        end
      when None
        ()
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  None
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let nums = parse_ints(line1)
let target = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
when Some(v)
  v
when None
  0
end

match find_pair(nums, target)
when Some(s)
  print("found=#{s}")
when None
  print("none")
end
